reachability_artifacts: built sworn doctor and sworn doctor --fix runs against isolated legacy, corrupt, conflicting, interrupted, and canonical temporary homes, recording exit codes, redacted output, file modes, and hashes
built provider-only command run with a corrupt account.json, recording successful provider resolution and zero account-file mutation

effort_complexity.rationale: See spec.json; the implementer must confirm the migration kill-point inventory against live filesystem call sites.

release_benefit: A SwornAgent user can use account identity without losing provider credentials, changing model routes, purchasing managed inference, or generating unapproved outbound traffic.

positive_scenarios: A legacy combined record migrates to a versioned provider record with every provider and unrelated value intact, no account record, and fresh-login guidance.
A provider-only command continues to work when account state is absent or invalid.
negative_scenarios: An interrupted migration cannot delete the only recoverable copy of a provider key or leave a token-bearing backup behind.
A corrupt, conflicting, or future-version record cannot be silently normalized or overwritten.
validation.benefit_hypothesis: Separating versioned ownership removes the shared corruption and logout blast radius that made account use unsafe for local provider users.

design_decisions.choice: Keep credentials.json provider-owned and move account sessions to account.json.
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified 2026-07-15: separate provider-owned credentials.json and session-owned account.json.
design_decisions.rationale: Different secret domains require different lifecycles and corruption boundaries.

design_decisions.choice: Use automatic verify-and-replace migration and discard issuer-less legacy session fields.
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified 2026-07-15: preserve and verify provider state, create no account record from issuer-less legacy fields, discard those fields at atomic replacement, and require fresh login.
design_decisions.rationale: The upgrade should be turnkey without guessing token origin or allowing a partial write to destroy provider secrets.
